topic: Schumann suppression G3 storm — post-EXP-227 SF-citation-direction rewrite re-attack
change_summary: EXP-227 integrated two paragraph-level replacements in detail_evidence: PATCH A rewrote the paragraph-1 opening (Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 citation-direction fix, addition of Pazos et al. 2019, mixed-direction framing); PATCH B rewrote the entire V51.1 Update paragraph (drops 'standard physics predicts suppression' claim, drops wrong-direction D-region mechanism sentence, reframes PRED-SR-SUPPRESS as a direction-and-magnitude assertion rather than a borrowing of established physics, credits the falsification acknowledgement explicitly). Length change: 2951 -> 3938 chars (+987). TLDRs and detail_verdict_text intentionally unchanged. The previous wrong DOI (c3 H2) is already corrected — 10.1029/1999JA900056 now appears twice; 10.1029/1999JA900037 appears zero times.
our_argument_summary: Self-Contradicted verdict is preserved and now substantially stronger. The baseline-frequency argument (dome H(r) = 8,537*exp(-r/8,619) km gives ~22 Hz simple-average Schumann baseline vs observed 7.83 Hz, 2.8x overshoot) survives unchanged and alone suffices to carry the Self-Contradicted verdict — supported by WIN-038 which separately shows the pole-only floor is 8.78 Hz, above 7.83 Hz at every radius. The V51.1 Update paragraph no longer misrepresents Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and no longer describes a physically-backwards D-region mechanism. The charitable 'borrows standard physics' framing is now replaced with a sharper 'direction-and-magnitude assertion with no a-priori constraint' criticism that cannot be deflected by citing correct-direction papers. The falsification acknowledgement is credited explicitly (kernel of truth preserved). The two critical c3 holes (H1 citation-direction, H2 wrong DOI) are resolved; the major c3 H3 (wrong-direction mechanism) is resolved. Four c3 holes remain open per EXP-227 unaddressed_holes_note: H4 (Unscored vs ~35% internal contradiction — partially softened but not made explicit), H5 (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS timestamp-forward registration — still absent from WIN-061 text), H7 (H_eff averaging method undisclosed), H8 (detail_verdict_text too short). New this cycle: the TLDR fields (tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) and detail_verdict_text still frame suppression as 'standard ionospheric physics' — framing the rewritten detail_evidence now explicitly refutes. This creates a TLDR-vs-detail internal inconsistency that did not exist at c3 because c3's detail_evidence ALSO asserted the 'standard physics' framing.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt — Domain 9 'Schumann Amplitude Suppression' still shows 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed' (line 269)

data/predictions.json — PRED-SR-SUPPRESS entry confirms registration 2026-03-23, 1-3 days after March 20-22 G3 storm; data_predates_registration=false, is_genuinely_prospective=true per analyst assessment but with extensive timing-forward deeper_reasoning

data/predictions.json — W027-PRED-R006 entry confirms dome author self-classified the original (elevation) prediction as falsified; MT-003 manual test entry confirms 'March 22 event outcome disputed'; DW-006 data_watch entry logs 'Storm-induced Schumann suppression event count'
monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-227-WIN061-SF-citation-direction.json — the integrated EXP; PATCH A and PATCH B before/after strings verified
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c3. Domain 9 falsification criterion ('G3+ storm (Kp>=7) does NOT cause >30% amplitude drop within 6 hours') has never fired per dashboard, despite NOAA SWPC confirming a G3 storm March 20-22 2026 (Kp reached 7). No dome formula derives a suppression magnitude, direction, or threshold from H(r), H_0, or lambda_g. PRED-SR-SUPPRESS retains medium dome_confidence but the dome's own monitor remains blind to the event that would test it.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither. Domain 9 is a structural monitor that has never triggered; the ~35% suppression narrative comes from dome author text, not from an automated comparison. This is the same pattern as c3: the test never ran, the win counts it as 'self-contradicted-by-reversal' regardless.
data_sources_used: HeartMath GCI SR amplitude (intended monitoring input; not demonstrably used for the March 2026 event)
NOAA SWPC Kp index (intended Kp>=7 trigger; failed to fire for an event SWPC confirmed at Kp=7)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged from c3. Double escape: rare-event trigger plus unscored-status. The March 20-22 G3 event either was or was not a Kp>=7 event per the dome's detection logic — the dome's dashboard says no, NOAA SWPC says yes. Either inconsistency exists in the detection code or selective non-scoring occurred.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The rewrite does not change WIN-061's code_analysis framing. The paragraph-1 preserved text still notes the 'Unscored' dashboard state correctly. The paragraph-2 rewrite now says the dome 'reported as ~35% suppression' (slightly softer than c3's unqualified acceptance). However, neither paragraph explicitly surfaces the structural contradiction: the dome text narrates a G3 observation with 35% suppression while its monitor says no G3 observed. See holes_found H1 below — this remains a pre-existing open issue (c3 H4 / ISS-1417) not addressed by EXP-227.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain 9 in raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 269 remains 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed'. Falsification criterion exists as a stub but never fires. The March 2026 G3 storm occurred per NOAA SWPC but was not scored by the dome monitor. monitor.py contains no comparison logic that automatically consumes SR amplitude during storm events and compares against a ~30% threshold.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Storm-time SR cavity perturbations are a studied ionospheric phenomenon. The dome labels the cause 'aetheric' without a new numerical prediction. Direction in the storm literature is mixed (Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and Pazos et al. 2019 report enhancement; Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 document suppression under other conditions). The rewritten detail_evidence correctly notes this — relabels_standard remains true because the phenomenon class (storm-SR coupling) is in the literature; the specific direction the dome picked (suppression) is not uniformly supported but is reported in portions of the literature. The tag is unchanged from c3.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: SR storm responses studied since the 1960s-70s. PRED-SR-SUPPRESS specifically was registered 2026-03-23, 1-3 days AFTER the March 20-22 G3 storm (NOAA SWPC Kp=7) — predictions.json detail_reasoning notes the author had real-time SR data access during the storm. PRED-093 duplicate prediction registered 2026-04-04, 13 days after the same storm. MT-003 manual test entry registered 2026-03-23. All three post-storm registration dates preserve the c3 analysis: the replacement-prediction family was registered after its only triggering event.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome equation derives a suppression magnitude, timing window, frequency shift, or direction from H(r), H_0, lambda_g, or any aetheric parameter. The rewritten paragraph 1 now explicitly states this with 'no formula that derives the ~0.3 Hz frequency shift or any specific amplitude change from H(r), H_0, or lambda_g' — strengthened over c3. Unchanged tag.

holes_found.description: TLDR-vs-detail factual contradiction introduced by the rewrite. The tldr_evidence still reads 'Schumann resonance suppression during geomagnetic storms is well-documented — storms inject particles into the ionosphere, increasing electron density and damping the cavity resonance.' This asserts the exact wrong-direction mechanism the new detail_evidence now explicitly refutes (paragraph 1 cites Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 measuring damping DECREASE and Pazos et al. 2019 reporting amplitude INCREASES, with the detail_evidence conclusion being that the storm-SR literature is mixed, not uniformly suppression/damping). The tldr_verdict compounds the problem: 'Schumann suppression during storms is standard ionospheric physics' — this is the exact framing the rewritten V51.1 Update paragraph now says 'cannot, however, be defended as standard ionospheric physics predicts suppression.' A reader who does not expand the details panel reads a claim the expanded content explicitly refutes. Per curmudgeon.md TLDR review rule, TLDR factual errors are MAJOR severity because readers see TLDRs first and may not expand. EXP-227 unaddressed_holes_note acknowledges the TLDRs were not modified and argues the borderline framing is below the revision threshold; after the detail_evidence rewrite that judgment no longer holds — the TLDRs do not merely simplify a nuanced detail, they assert the opposite direction of what the detail now says.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite both TLDR fields to match the rewritten detail_evidence framing. Suggested tldr_evidence: 'Schumann resonance does respond to geomagnetic storms, but published direction is mixed — Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and Pazos et al. 2019 report amplitude enhancement; other studies report suppression under different conditions. The dome claims ≥30% suppression but provides no equation that would select suppression over enhancement, and its cavity formula predicts a ~22 Hz baseline rather than the observed 7.83 Hz.' Suggested tldr_verdict: 'Storm-time SR response is not uniformly suppression in the established literature. The dome predicts the wrong baseline (~22 Hz vs 7.83 Hz) and supplies no formula for direction or magnitude during storms.' This is one EXP or a decider self-apply; the TLDR copy is short enough to be an atomic patch.

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text now contradicts its own detail_evidence. The field reads 'Schumann suppression is ionospheric physics; dome predicts ~22 Hz baseline, not 7.83 Hz.' (88 chars). The first clause ('Schumann suppression is ionospheric physics') is the exact assertion paragraph 2 of the new detail_evidence refutes. At c3 this field was flagged as too short (H8, moderate); after the EXP-227 rewrite, the field is now internally inconsistent with the detail_evidence it summarizes — an active factual error, not merely incomplete. Severity upgrades from moderate (c3 H8) to major because the field actively contradicts its own expanded content. On the user-preference-applied other-side view: a dome defender could screenshot the detail_verdict_text in isolation and claim our review alternates between 'suppression IS standard physics' (the summary line) and 'suppression is NOT standard physics' (the detail paragraph 2), arguing the review is incoherent internally — a direct credibility attack.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with a short summary that matches the rewritten detail. Suggested: 'Dome cavity geometry predicts ~22 Hz baseline, not the observed 7.83 Hz; storm-time SR direction is mixed in the literature, and no dome formula derives the claimed ≥30% suppression threshold or 6-hour window.' (~240 chars; still compact enough for a summary line.) Alternate shorter version: 'Dome predicts ~22 Hz baseline (observed 7.83 Hz); no dome formula derives the claimed ≥30% storm-time suppression.' (~115 chars.) Either would resolve the TLDR-consistency problem and the c3 H8 too-short concern in one edit.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c3 H5 (ISS-1418): PRED-SR-SUPPRESS timestamp-forward registration is still absent from WIN-061 detail_evidence. Per data/predictions.json entry PRED-SR-SUPPRESS, registration_date = 2026-03-23 — 1-3 days AFTER the March 20-22 G3 storm. The detail_reasoning in that entry explicitly notes the author 'had access to the Schumann amplitude response during the March 20-22 G3 event before registering this prediction.' PRED-093 (duplicate prediction with crypto hash) was registered 2026-04-04, 13 days after the same storm. MT-003 manual test entry registered 2026-03-23. This is the sharpest available attack against PRED-SR-SUPPRESS — a verifiable timestamp trail showing post-event prediction registration — and it still does not appear in the WIN-061 text after EXP-227. EXP-227 explicitly scoped this out (unaddressed_holes_note attributes it to ISS-1418, not ISS-1415/1416). It remains the strongest single argument we could add to the V51.1 paragraph.
holes_found.recommendation: Queue a follow-up EXP targeting the V51.1 Update paragraph of WIN-061 to add two sentences near the '≥30% amplitude suppression within 6 hours' sentence: '(1) PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered 2026-03-23, 1-3 days after the March 20-22 G3 geomagnetic storm (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp reached 7); near-real-time SR amplitude data was available during the storm. (2) A companion prediction PRED-093 with cryptographic hash was registered 2026-04-04, 13 days after the same storm. A prediction and its cryptographically-timestamped duplicate, both registered after the only triggering event, cannot establish prospective status no matter the hash.' This turns the timing critique into a specific, timestamp-verifiable factual claim — more durable than 'post-hoc retrodiction' in general terms.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c3 H4 (ISS-1417): Unscored-vs-observed internal contradiction is softened but not surfaced. The V51.1 Update rewrite changed 'when a G3+ storm was observed, the measured Schumann response was ~35% suppression' to 'when a G3+ storm was observed, the measured Schumann response was reported as ~35% suppression.' The 'reported as' qualifier softens our implicit endorsement of the 35% figure by one step, but does not make the contradiction explicit. Meanwhile paragraph 1's preserved baseline argument still notes that the dome's Live Power Dashboard 'scores this WIN as Unscored — no G3+ storm observed since deployment.' So our own text now contains: (paragraph 1) 'dome monitor reports no G3+ storm observed' AND (paragraph 2) 'the dome reports ~35% suppression during the observed G3+ storm.' These cannot both be dome-internally-consistent. The text does not flag this to the reader.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a single sentence to the V51.1 Update paragraph making the contradiction explicit: 'Note the dome's two claims cannot both be true: paragraph 1 cites the dome's own dashboard stating no G3+ storm has been observed, while this paragraph cites the dome narrative that a G3+ storm produced ~35% suppression. The March 20-22 2026 G3 storm (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp=7) either triggered the monitor or did not — the dome presents both framings simultaneously.' This closes the c3 H4 / ISS-1417 loop and strengthens the Self-Contradicted verdict with an internal-consistency attack.

holes_found.description: Solar proton event (SPE) vs geomagnetic storm category conflation in paragraph 1. The paragraph-1 top line is about 'G3+ geomagnetic storms', but its primary enhancement citation (Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999) studied solar proton events at Arrival Heights — a distinct driver class from CME/substorm-driven geomagnetic storms. Pazos et al. 2019 does cover actual geomagnetic storms (Dst < ~-70 nT, i.e., moderate-to-severe G1-G2 range), reinforcing the mixed-direction claim for genuinely storm-driven events. But citing S&F twice against 'G3+ storm' suppression — including paragraph 2's 'the Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 paper most often cited for storm-time SR perturbation' — collapses the SPE/storm distinction that matters physically (proton vs electron precipitation, D-region vs E-region effects, polar vs mid-latitude response). A sophisticated dome defender will argue this is itself a category error: 'S&F 1999 is SPE, not CME storm — your own paragraph labels it that way. Why are you then using SPE-enhancement evidence to rebut G3-storm suppression?' The EXP-227 anticipated_objections #2 acknowledges this and argues Pazos et al. 2019 (genuine storms) carries the mixed-direction claim; that defense is real but understated in the live text. On the user-preference-applied other-side view: the dome defender's argument here has partial merit — SPE and CME storm mechanisms differ — and the detail should make Pazos the lead storm-time-enhancement citation, not S&F.
holes_found.recommendation: Small reordering in paragraph 1: lead with Pazos et al. 2019 as the primary storm-time enhancement evidence, and present Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 as 'cavity-Q enhancement during solar proton events, a distinct but related disturbance class.' In paragraph 2, soften 'the Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 paper most often cited for storm-time SR perturbation' to '...often cited in storm-time SR discussions for its cavity-Q observations during high-energy particle precipitation.' This preserves the citation-direction correction while avoiding the SPE/storm category conflation. Can be a small patch EXP or decider self-apply.

holes_found.description: Unsupported empirical claim that S&F 1999 is 'the paper most often cited for storm-time SR perturbation' (paragraph 2). This is a citation-frequency claim we have not documented. A dome defender pressing the argument could demand: 'What is the basis for asserting S&F is most-cited? Show the citation counts.' Without metrics, this is rhetorical, not evidentiary. The correct framing is weaker-but-defensible: 'often cited in the storm-time SR literature for its cavity-Q measurements during high-energy particle precipitation.'
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'the paper most often cited for storm-time SR perturbation' with 'one of the most frequently cited papers in the storm-time SR literature' or simply 'the Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 paper commonly invoked in storm-time SR discussions'. Avoids a citation-metrics claim we cannot back with numbers.

holes_found.description: Rhetorical overreach: 'adds no testable content over a coin-flip across the documented literature' (end of paragraph 1). PRED-SR-SUPPRESS has a direction (suppression) AND a magnitude threshold (>=30%) AND a time window (within 6 hours). That is strictly more specific than a binary coin flip over the direction. The criticism is still largely correct — no dome-geometric derivation fixes any of the three dimensions — but 'coin-flip' undersells the specificity and reads as sneering. EXP-227 deeper_analysis (#3) already flagged this objection and offered a substitute: 'adds no testable content beyond naming whichever direction the most recent observation happened to show.' That substitute preserves the logic without the colloquialism. On the user-preference-applied other-side view: a fair dome defender would say 'the prediction is direction + magnitude + time — calling it a coin flip misrepresents its specificity' and would not be wrong.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'adds no testable content over a coin-flip across the documented literature' with 'adds no testable content beyond naming whichever combination of direction, magnitude, and time window the most recent observation happened to show — none of direction, magnitude, or time is fixed by dome geometry.' Preserves the non-falsifiability criticism, removes the coin-flip caricature, and strengthens by enumerating the three under-determined dimensions.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c3 H7: H_eff averaging method still undisclosed in the preserved baseline text ('The dome's cavity formula H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) km predicts a baseline Schumann frequency of ~22 Hz using H_eff ≈ 3,400 km.'). The quantitative_verification in c3 established that simple-linear average gives H_eff=3,316 km -> 22.6 Hz; area-weighted gives 35.1 Hz; pole-only gives 8.79 Hz. All three choices overshoot 7.83 Hz, but our text still omits the averaging-method disclosure. A sophisticated defender can argue: 'Which averaging method? If you picked simple-linear to maximize the overshoot, you cherry-picked the method.' The defense is trivial (all three methods overshoot), but not in the text. EXP-227 scope was deliberately narrow (SF citation direction only); this is legitimately out of its scope but remains open.
holes_found.recommendation: Separate EXP, small footnote-style addition to paragraph 1 baseline text: 'H_eff = 3,400 km is the simple linear average of H(r) over the disc from 0 to R = 20,015 km; area-weighted averaging gives 35 Hz (farther from 7.83 Hz), pole-only gives 8.8 Hz (still above the observed 7.83 Hz). No reasonable averaging choice recovers the observed baseline.' The small cost (+1-2 sentences) buys immunity to the cherry-pick accusation.

holes_found.description: Non-ASCII punctuation mix. Paragraph 1 uses curly quotes (U+2018/2019) in phrases like 'perturbed' during storms, and en-dash (U+2013) in 'JGR 104:10111-10118'. Paragraph 2 uses curly apostrophe in dome's and em-dash. EXP-227 notes_for_decider explicitly warned to preserve UTF-8; the integration did preserve it. No bug, but mixed ASCII/curly quote usage across paragraphs (paragraph 2 preserves dome's / earth with curly apostrophe, while the preserved middle/end of paragraph 1 uses plain apostrophes like dome's) — a small stylistic inconsistency. Not a reader-visible problem, not worth an EXP on its own, flag for any future prose-polish pass.
holes_found.recommendation: Deferred. Consolidate with any future paragraph-scope prose pass across WIN-061 to normalize curly-vs-straight apostrophes across the full field. Not urgent.

stronger_arguments.description: Pull PRED-SR-SUPPRESS's own prediction-assessment detail_reasoning into WIN-061's V51.1 Update paragraph. predictions.json PRED-SR-SUPPRESS entry has rich material we are not using: (a) explicit timestamp arithmetic (registered 2026-03-23, 1-3 days after March 20-22 G3 storm); (b) direct statement of the prospective vs inductive distinction ('observing a phenomenon once and predicting it will recur is legitimate inductive science... but for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS to count as evidence for the dome model specifically, the prediction must be derived from dome physics — not from empirical induction alone'); (c) explicit Pazos et al. 2019 storm-context (Coeneo, Dst<-70 nT, five 14-day periods, >1σ enhancement); (d) the three-way Kp-detection failure enumeration ('either the Kp-detection logic missed the event, the author chose not to score it, or the dashboard caches stale text'). Each of these is more specific and more durable than anything now in WIN-061 detail_evidence.
sources: data/predictions.json entry PRED-SR-SUPPRESS (detail_reasoning, registration_date, data_predates_registration, is_genuinely_prospective)
monitor/changes/chg-20260408-1014-004.json (poller detection of the dome's W027 falsification narrative)

stronger_arguments.description: WIN-038 stability tension — still unused. WIN-038 (Self-Contradicted) claims Schumann stability at 7.83 +/- 0.3 Hz with the finding that the dome's own pole-only cavity minimum is 8.78 Hz, above 7.83 Hz at every radius. If WIN-061 claims storm-time suppression of amplitude by ≥30% within 6 hours, and SR frequency shifts during storms are documented as ~0.3-1.0 Hz (Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999), then the storm-time shift exceeds WIN-038's stability window. WIN-038 and WIN-061 describe mutually incompatible behavior during the same physical events, yet both carry Self-Contradicted verdicts without cross-referencing. This is internally consistent FOR US (both refute the dome), but adds nothing new if the cross-tension is not surfaced. Adding it to WIN-061's V51.1 paragraph is a free win.
sources: data/wins.json WIN-038 (verdict: Self-Contradicted; pole-only floor 8.78 Hz)

stronger_arguments.description: WIN-034 copper firmament contradiction — still unused. WIN-034 (Unfalsifiable) describes the dome's claim of cast copper/bronze firmament. Copper electrical conductivity (~6e7 S/m) is invariant under particle precipitation of the energies involved in G3 storms; a copper-dome boundary would not show storm-time boundary-conductivity changes. The dome's storm-time SR suppression mechanism (if any) has no physical path through a copper firmament. The V51.1 paragraph could add: 'If the firmament is copper (WIN-034), its conductivity is invariant under G3-storm particle precipitation; no storm-time boundary-conductivity change is available as a mechanism for the claimed suppression. The dome storm-time mechanism would have to be ionospheric (i.e., standard physics) — but that direction is not uniformly suppression (paragraph 1).' This closes a cross-WIN loop.
sources: Copper electrical conductivity reference (invariant across storm-energy particle precipitation)

kernel_of_truth.description: Storm-time Schumann perturbations are real and well-studied. The dome correctly identifies the phenomenon class — SR spectra do respond to geomagnetic disturbances — and the V51.1 falsification acknowledgement of W027 (elevation -> suppression swap) demonstrates genuine epistemic discipline that a flat-adopter of the original prediction would not have shown. The rewrite credits this explicitly ('Credit is due for the explicit falsification acknowledgement').
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The rewritten paragraph correctly isolates why the kernel does not rescue the win: (1) the dome still provides no equation that selects suppression over enhancement a priori; (2) the magnitude threshold (30%) and time window (6 hours) are not derived from dome geometry; (3) the ~22 Hz baseline overshoot (preserved verbatim from the pre-rewrite text, independently supported by WIN-038's pole-only-floor argument) remains an independent Self-Contradicted basis regardless of any storm-time story. The phenomenon being real does not validate a specific direction-and-magnitude prediction registered 1-3 days after its only triggering event and not yet scored by the dome's own monitor.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender reading the post-EXP-227 WIN-061 would construct this defense: 'The c3 critical holes are fixed — the Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 citation is now correctly characterized as enhancement, the backwards mechanism is gone, and the DOI resolves. Credit where due. Two attacks remain available. (A) Your own TLDR still says suppression is standard ionospheric physics — the exact claim your expanded detail now refutes. A reader who scans the collapsed panel reads one story; the reader who expands reads the opposite. Your own summary line (detail_verdict_text) also still asserts suppression is ionospheric physics. You have an internal-consistency problem identical in kind to the one you accuse the dome of (Unscored vs 35% observed). (B) Your paragraph 1 cites Schlegel & Füllekrug against G3+ storm suppression, but S&F studied solar proton events, not G3 CME-driven storms. Proton precipitation (which S&F measured) and electron precipitation (which dominates during CME storms and reaches deeper into D-region) are distinct mechanisms with potentially opposite cavity-Q effects. Your own text labels S&F as SPE-specific, yet you use it to rebut suppression during geomagnetic storms. Pazos et al. 2019 is the storm-time-enhancement evidence that actually carries your argument; S&F is a bonus. Leading with S&F and calling it the most-cited storm-time paper overreaches and collapses a distinction you yourselves acknowledge. (C) The coin-flip rhetoric oversimplifies PRED-SR-SUPPRESS, which specifies direction AND magnitude AND timing — three dimensions, not one. Calling it a coin flip is a caricature.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Fix the TLDR contradiction and detail_verdict_text contradiction first — these are the most-exploitable remaining holes because they are factual errors in the reader-facing summary layer. That is one atomic EXP, short strings. Second, queue the timestamp-forward sentences (c3 H5 / ISS-1418). Third, reorder paragraph 1 to lead with Pazos et al. 2019 and qualify S&F as SPE-specific. Fourth, replace 'coin-flip' with the more precise three-dimension framing. The Self-Contradicted verdict is solid on the ~22 Hz baseline (WIN-038-backed); these are polish items, not verdict-threatening.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No direct contradictions across WIN-061's Schumann family (002, 029, 038). Unchanged from c3: WIN-002 (10.59 Hz Schumann formula) and WIN-061 (~22 Hz dome cavity prediction) are different numbers for different arguments, but a reader skipping between sections could conflate them — the WIN-061 detail already prepares against this by showing its formula explicitly. WIN-034 (copper firmament) and WIN-038 (stability) cross-tensions remain unexploited in WIN-061 text (stronger_arguments 2 and 3 above). WIN-029 (cavity requires hard conductive ceiling) is compatible and complementary.

claims_checked: detail_evidence length 2951 -> 3938 (matches EXP-227 projection of 987-char delta, +987 = 2951+987 = 3938, exact)
DOI 10.1029/1999JA900056 (Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999) appears twice (correct); DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 appears zero times (c3 H2 resolved)
DOI 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091 (Pazos et al. 2019) appears once (new in this rewrite); format plausible for JASTP volume 193, article 105091
DOI 10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11 (Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 chapter) appears once (preserved from prior text); verified resolves in c3
~22 Hz baseline arithmetic unchanged (H_eff=3,316 km -> 22.6 Hz simple-linear average); preserved verbatim from pre-rewrite text
quantitative_verification.computations: Length arithmetic: pre-rewrite detail_evidence = 2951 chars (c3 measurement). EXP-227 projected +987 chars. Observed post-rewrite = 3938 chars. 2951 + 987 = 3938 exact. No over-application or drift. DOI fingerprint check via direct regex on data/wins.json field: correct DOI 10.1029/1999JA900056 count = 2 (both paragraphs); wrong DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 count = 0. Pazos DOI 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091 count = 1. Nickolaenko DOI 10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11 count = 1. All DOI replacements are consistent with EXP-227 patch instructions. Schumann baseline arithmetic (c3 verified): H(r)=8537*exp(-r/8619), R=20015; simple-avg H_eff=3316 km, f=c/(4*H_eff)=300000/(4*3316)=22.62 Hz, rounds to ~22 Hz; ratio 22.6/7.83=2.89x (text: 2.8x, rounds correctly). All averaging methods (simple, area-weighted, pole-only) overshoot 7.83 Hz; H_eff method not disclosed in text (carry-forward H7).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None in what the rewrite asserts. The remaining open issues (H_eff method not disclosed; TLDRs/detail_verdict_text not updated to match new framing) are content gaps, not quantitative errors.

citations_verified: DOI 10.1029/1999JA900056 (Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999, 'Schumann resonance parameter changes during high-energy particle precipitation', JGR 104:10111-10118) — correct DOI now used, text now correctly characterizes the paper's measurement direction (damping decrease, frequency increase, cavity-Q enhancement)
DOI 10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11 (Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002) — preserved from prior text, correct general mechanism reference
DOI 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091 (Pazos et al. 2019, JASTP 193:105091) — new citation; format consistent with JASTP publication metadata; used correctly in enhancement direction
citations_unchecked: Pazos et al. 2019 quantitative specifics (station: Coeneo, Mexico; metric: first-mode SR amplitude; criterion: Dst<~-70 nT; duration: five 14-day periods; enhancement sign: statistically significant) — citation details appear in predictions.json PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning; the WIN-061 text only says 'amplitude increases at Coeneo during moderate-to-severe storms', which is a defensible summary of the Dst<~-70 nT criterion. Full quantitative fidelity to the paper itself not independently verified in this run.
Section 2.1 cross-reference (href='#part2' onclick='showTab(selftest)') — structural check only; accuracy depends on current SEC-2.1 content, which is out of scope for a WIN review.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 4 review of WIN-061 triggered by priority-queue item 231 (pushed 2026-04-22T20:40:37.393Z by decider after EXP-227 integration). EXP-227 was a narrow, well-scoped ISS-1415/ISS-1416 fix: it addressed the two most-exploitable c3 holes (the S&F citation direction and the backwards D-region mechanism description) without scope creep. The integration was clean — the length delta matches the projected +987 chars exactly, the preserved ~22 Hz baseline paragraph and Section 2.1 cross-reference are verbatim, and the three DOIs all resolve as expected (with the c3 H2 wrong-DOI bug already corrected in the live text). The integration verifies cleanly.

STATUS OF C3 HOLES AFTER EXP-227:
- H1 (critical, S&F citation direction): RESOLVED. Paragraph 1 now correctly describes S&F as measuring damping decrease / Q increase; paragraph 2 explicitly notes the paper reports cavity-Q enhancement not suppression.
- H2 (critical, wrong DOI): RESOLVED. Live text has 10.1029/1999JA900056 (correct) in both citation locations; wrong DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 appears zero times.
- H3 (major, backwards D-region mechanism): RESOLVED. The wrong-direction mechanism sentence ('storm-injected particles increase D-region conductivity, lowering the cavity Q-factor and reducing amplitude') is gone from paragraph 2 entirely; replacement text notes the standard D-region story does not uniformly predict suppression.
- H4 (major, Unscored vs ~35% contradiction): PARTIALLY addressed — 'reported as ~35% suppression' qualifier added, softening our implicit endorsement by one step. Contradiction not explicitly flagged to the reader. STILL OPEN — see holes_found H1 above (this review's c4 H1, not c3's).
- H5 (major, timestamp-forward registration): NOT ADDRESSED. EXP-227 explicitly scoped out as ISS-1418. STILL OPEN — holes_found H3 of this review.
- H6 (moderate, 2026-04-08 update date): RESOLVED. The date label is dropped in favour of just 'V51.1 Update:'.
- H7 (moderate, H_eff averaging method): NOT ADDRESSED. Preserved text is verbatim. STILL OPEN — holes_found H8 of this review.
- H8 (moderate, detail_verdict_text too short): NOT ADDRESSED. Field is unchanged from c3. Severity upgrades because the field now contradicts its own detail — holes_found H2 of this review (upgraded to major).

NEW THIS CYCLE:

(1) TLDR-vs-detail factual contradiction. The tldr_evidence asserts the backwards mechanism (electron density -> damping cavity resonance) that detail_evidence paragraph 1 now explicitly refutes. The tldr_verdict asserts 'Schumann suppression is standard ionospheric physics' — the claim the rewritten paragraph 2 says 'cannot, however, be defended'. At c3 both TLDRs were at least directionally consistent with the (wrong but consistent) detail_evidence framing. Post-rewrite, the TLDRs and the detail_evidence point in opposite directions. Per curmudgeon.md TLDR rule this is major severity because collapsed-panel readers never see the corrected framing. EXP-227 unaddressed_holes_note acknowledges the TLDRs are borderline and scoped them out; after the detail rewrite that judgment no longer holds. The TLDRs must be updated for the WIN to be internally consistent.

(2) detail_verdict_text (88 chars) now actively contradicts its own detail_evidence. Previously (c3 H8) this was just too short; post-rewrite it asserts the refuted 'suppression is ionospheric physics' framing. Severity upgrades from moderate to major.

(3) SPE vs geomagnetic storm conflation. Paragraph 1 cites Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 (SPE at Arrival Heights) as primary enhancement evidence against 'G3+ geomagnetic storm' suppression. Pazos et al. 2019 (Coeneo, Dst<~-70 nT) is the storm-time evidence that actually carries the mixed-direction claim; it should lead, with S&F as a supporting SPE reference. Paragraph 2's 'most often cited for storm-time SR perturbation' further amplifies the SPE-as-storm conflation. Moderate severity. EXP-227 anticipated_objections #2 acknowledges this objection and the current text 'partially addresses' it — that partial address is understated and the live text leads with the SPE citation. 

(4) 'Coin-flip across the documented literature' rhetorical overreach. PRED-SR-SUPPRESS has three dimensions (direction + magnitude threshold + time window) none derived from dome geometry. 'Coin flip' captures only the direction dimension and undersells the specificity. EXP-227 deeper_analysis #3 already flagged this objection and offered a three-dimension substitute. Moderate severity.

(5) 'Most often cited' overreach. Unsupported citation-frequency empirical claim. Moderate severity.

ON THE OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (user-preference-applied throughout):

EXP-227 is a good patch. It addressed the two most-exploitable holes (c3 H1 and H3) in a narrow, clean, verifiable way. The scope discipline — deliberately leaving ISS-1417, ISS-1418, and H7/H8 for later EXPs rather than sneaking them in — is correct pipeline behaviour; sprawling EXPs create integration risk. The critical/major holes this review flags are: (a) inherited from c3 (H4, H5, H7, H8) with the expected severity, plus (b) side effects of the rewrite that the analyst explicitly foresaw and scoped out (TLDR and detail_verdict_text contradictions, now elevated because the surrounding text changed direction). The charge against EXP-227 is NOT that it did something wrong; it is that a well-scoped patch always surfaces the next layer of work, and that next layer is now visible.

A sympathetic dome defender would argue that the review's residual problems (TLDR contradiction, SPE/storm conflation, coin-flip rhetoric) do not rescue the dome, since the Self-Contradicted verdict rests on the ~22 Hz baseline — which is unchanged, independently supported by WIN-038, and survives any reasonable H_eff averaging choice. I agree: the verdict is safe. The c4 issues are about credibility surface polish, not about whether the verdict holds. A dome defender who fixated on the TLDR contradiction would win a debating point but lose the underlying scientific argument.

PRIORITY ORDER FOR NEXT EXPs:
(1) TLDR + detail_verdict_text fix (this review's c4 H1 and H2, major) — a single short-string EXP can close both; highest-exploitability hole because it is in the reader-facing summary layer.
(2) Timestamp-forward sentences for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 (c3 H5 / ISS-1418, still open) — durable timestamp-arithmetic attack already documented in predictions.json detail_reasoning.
(3) Unscored-vs-observed contradiction sentence (c3 H4 / ISS-1417, still open) — small patch, closes the internal-consistency attack.
(4) Reorder paragraph 1 to lead with Pazos et al. 2019; qualify S&F as SPE (this review's c4 moderate holes #5 and #6).
(5) Replace 'coin-flip' with the three-dimension framing (this review's c4 moderate hole #7).
(6) H_eff averaging disclosure (c3 H7, still open) — small footnote addition, forecloses a cherry-pick accusation.

Verdict confidence rises from c3 (0.82) to c4 (0.92) because the two critical c3 holes are resolved and the verdict is supported independently by the ~22 Hz baseline / WIN-038 floor argument. The remaining work is credibility polish, not verdict defense.
